steps.title: Apply the vertical translation rule
steps.content:
Translating a graph up $k$ units transforms the function by adding $k$ to its value. Since the graph of $y = g(x)$ is the result of translating $y = f(x)$ up 3 units, we have:
$$g(x) = f(x) + 3$$
To find $g(0)$, we need:
$$g(0) = f(0) + 3$$

steps.title: Calculate the product and find g(0)
steps.content:
Multiply the factors step by step:
$$(-8)(-5) = 40$$
$$40 \cdot (-3) = -120$$
So $f(0) = -120$. Therefore:
$$g(0) = f(0) + 3 = -120 + 3 = -117$$
